entity_name: Top 21 Industrial Construction Companies in Georgia
canonical_name: Top 21 Industrial Construction Companies in Georgia
facts: Industrial construction in Georgia focuses on building infrastructure and facilities crucial for manufacturing, energy, and logistics sectors.
The highlighted companies range in size from small firms to large enterprises, employing anywhere from 10 to over 1,900 individuals.
These companies were founded between 1945 and 2022 and specialize in areas such as structural steel fabrication, concrete work, and project management.
Headquarters span various cities like Alpharetta, Atlanta, and Commerce, indicating strategic geographic distribution across the Southeast.
The drive for modernization in Georgia's construction sector encourages greater collaboration among firms to foster innovation and redefine project delivery methods.
SPG Construction, LLC is a private firm based in Commerce, Georgia, founded in 1985 with approximately 86 employees.
SPG Construction specializes in heavy industrial and process construction for chemicals, food production, and industrial water treatment.
SPG Construction emphasizes safety with a zero-injury goal on all projects and utilizes drones and iPads for project oversight.
Cives Steel Company is a private provider based in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 1951 with a headcount of 501-1,000.
Cives Steel specializes in structural steel fabrication and serves healthcare, commercial, airport, and stadium projects.
Cives Steel operates multiple fabrication facilities across the United States and was named the 2016 Small Manufacturer of the Year in Georgia.
C. A. Murren & Sons Company, Inc. is a construction and engineering firm based in Grayson, Georgia, founded in 1986 with approximately 191 employees.
C. A. Murren & Sons specializes in heavy structural concrete, steel erection, and marine construction for power, industrial, oil, gas, and water sectors.
C. A. Murren & Sons maintains offices in Georgia, Alabama, and Virginia to serve the Southeastern and Mid-Atlantic United States.
Dublin Construction Company Inc. is a family-owned firm based in Dublin, Georgia, founded in 1945 with approximately 61 employees.
Dublin Construction provides construction management, design-build, and self-perform services including steel fabrication and concrete work.
Dublin Construction has completed projects in manufacturing and industrial facilities and emphasizes transparency and quality workmanship.
Evans General Contractors is a private equity-owned firm based in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 2001 with approximately 410 professionals.
Evans General Contractors received latest funding in February 2017 and specializes in commercial construction for healthcare, manufacturing, and advanced technology.
Evans General Contractors has built specialized data centers and cold storage facilities while emphasizing innovative construction practices.
Carroll Daniel Construction is a family-owned company based in Gainesville, Georgia, founded in 1946 with approximately 285 employees.
Carroll Daniel serves automotive, education, healthcare, and manufacturing markets with services ranging from site selection to project management.
Carroll Daniel has partnered with Kubota Manufacturing and Enchem America on notable automotive sector projects and expanded offices to Nashville and Savannah.
Graycor Southern Inc. is a family-owned firm based in Kennesaw, Georgia, founded in 2014 with approximately 67 employees.
Graycor Southern provides project management and construction services for healthcare, retail, and industrial facilities, including manufacturing and logistics projects.
Kajima U.S.A. Inc. is a private construction and real estate development firm based in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 1961 with 1,001-5,000 employees.
Kajima U.S.A. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the global Kajima Corporation and provides engineering, architecture, and design-build solutions for industrial sectors.
Cleveland Electric Company is a specialty contractor based in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 1925 with approximately 395 employees.
Cleveland Electric specializes in electrical and mechanical construction for power generation, healthcare, and industrial applications using prefabrication and BIM.
Cleveland Electric has managed large-scale projects including the HJAIA New International Terminal and the South Cobb Tunnel Lift Station.
C.W. Matthews Contracting Co., Inc. is a heavy highway contractor based in Marietta, Georgia, founded in 1946 with over 1,900 employees.
C.W. Matthews specializes in asphalt paving, concrete construction, and grading, and reconstructed the I-16/I-75 interchange.
Reeves Young is a family-owned firm based in Sugar Hill, Georgia, founded in 1952 with approximately 318 employees.
Reeves Young's industrial division focuses on automotive and transportation projects, offering build-to-suit solutions and tenant improvements.
JBrennon Construction, Inc. is a certified small business and 100% Native American-owned firm based in Dalton, Georgia, founded in 2006 with 1-10 employees.
JBrennon Construction specializes in design-build, renovation, and project management for healthcare and retail sectors across 15 states.
Ordner Construction Company is a commercial general contractor based in Duluth, Georgia, founded in 1987 with approximately 63 employees.
Ordner Construction serves industrial, retail, medical, and office projects, with 93% of its business coming from repeat clients.
Ordner Construction has completed notable industrial projects including the Lakeland Dragstrip and various logistics centers.
SmithBuilt Construction Group is a private firm based in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 2022 with 1-10 employees.
SmithBuilt Construction focuses on education, municipal, healthcare, industrial, and commercial construction using CM at-risk and Hard Bid delivery methods.
Kevin Price Construction is a private firm based in Athens, Georgia, founded in 1989 with 17 employees.
Kevin Price Construction specializes in project management and design/build for educational, commercial, and large-scale industrial projects across Georgia, Alabama, and reciprocal states.
Pattillo Construction Corporation is a family-owned firm based in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 1950 with 11-50 employees.
Pattillo Construction specializes in design-build and construction management for manufacturing and distribution facilities, completing over 1,000 projects totaling over 80 million square feet.
Pattillo Construction is led by Bree Pattillo, the fourth generation of the family leadership.
C. Terry Hunt Industries, INC. is a private firm based in Valdosta, Georgia, founded in 1987 with 51-200 employees.
C. Terry Hunt Industries provides general contracting, engineering, and equipment rental for agriculture, chemicals, and energy sectors.
C. Terry Hunt Industries operates alongside sister companies Advanced Vessel and Alloy and Advanced Rental for metal fabrication and equipment rental.
Smith Construction, Inc. is a private firm based in Kingston, Georgia, founded in 1954 with 28 employees.
Smith Construction specializes in construction management and design/build for industrial clients, including food manufacturing facilities and environmental construction projects.
Noah Industrial LLC is a private firm based in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 2016 with 51-200 employees.
Noah Industrial specializes in mechanical, structural, and piping services for pulp and paper, power generation, and water treatment sectors.
CT Darnell Construction is a private firm based in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 1987 with approximately 27 employees.
CT Darnell Construction has been involved in over 1,000 projects and is licensed to operate in all 50 states.
LRA Constructors, Inc. is a private firm based in Albany, Georgia, founded in 1978 with 51-200 employees.
LRA Constructors serves automotive, healthcare, education, and industrial markets across the southeastern United States, with nearly 75% of work from referrals and repeat clients.
